description: The smallest legal journal record shape (iteration, action, hypothesis, gate, fingerprint, note, ts — the core fields the loop_journal.py module docstring documents, without any of the optional lineage fields) and the invariant that makes appending meaningful instead of just noisy: the SAME underlying bug, recurring a day later at a different line/hex/timestamp/duration, still fingerprints to the SAME value. That stability is why a new attempt is APPENDED, never rewriting record 1 — the fingerprint carries the identity of the failure across appends, not the line number.
harness: scripts/loop_journal.py:fingerprint(text) — the same pure function cmd_record() calls before appending.

checks.name: jsonl_parses
checks.detail: every non-blank line of journal.jsonl is valid JSON

checks.name: minimal_required_keys
checks.detail: every record has iteration, action, hypothesis, gate, fingerprint, note, ts

checks.name: iteration_monotonic
checks.detail: iteration values are non-decreasing top to bottom (append-only order)

checks.name: fingerprint_stable_across_recurrence
checks.detail: fingerprint(gate_output_1.txt) == fingerprint(gate_output_2.txt) == the fingerprint field recorded in BOTH journal rows, even though line number/hex/timestamp/duration all differ between the two raw failures

notes: Real records also carry a `commit` field (stamped by cmd_record for the `since` incremental-triage command) plus optional lineage fields (execution_state, stage_id, decision, retry_count, ...) — this fixture deliberately omits them to show the MINIMUM legal shape, not the full one (see fixtures/converge-success/.orchestrator/loop/journal.jsonl for a record with `commit`).
This file is genuinely append-only in production: cmd_record() only ever opens the journal with mode 'a' (scripts/loop_journal.py:cmd_record) — there is no update/rewrite verb in the CLI at all.
